reasoning: The government of Alberta recently introduced legislation (around May 2025) to lower the threshold for citizen-initiated referendums to 10% of eligible voters (approx. 177,000 signatures) and expanded the signature-gathering period to 120 days. Premier Danielle Smith explicitly stated that she has no plans for the government to initiate a referendum on independence, but will hold one in 2026 ONLY IF the citizen petition succeeds. Because the 120-day timeframe pushes the signature gathering phase well past the July 1, 2025 cutoff date—and because verification by Elections Alberta will require additional time—the petition process physically cannot be completed and verified before July 1. Consequently, an independence referendum cannot be "officially scheduled" by that date unless Smith makes a dramatic reversal and unilaterally bypasses her own newly introduced petition process. The likelihood of such a swift, severe reversal in politics over simply waiting for the petition mechanism she explicitly designed is extremely low. Therefore, the probability is roughly 3%.
